## "POWER FINANCE CORPORATION LIMITED, New Delhi"

Proposal For "Procurement of services for Design, Development, Implementation and Maintenance of PFC Website." Implementation and Maintenance of PFC Website. (No.: Bid Number: GEM/2025/B/63054098 &amp; Reference Number: 0209:197: 1:24-25: Website dated 04-June-2025)

|   SR. NO. | INDEX                                 | Page Ref.   |
|-----------|---------------------------------------|-------------|
|        1. | Covering Letter                       | A. 2        |
|        2. | COI, PAN, GST                         | B. 4        |
|        3. | Delhi Office proofs                   | C. 11       |
|        4. | CA Certificate and B. sheet           | D. 31       |
|        5. | a. COI                                | E. 43       |
|        6. | b. 3+ year old PO for Similar Exp     | F. 114      |
|        7. | Similar Work Experience               | G. 239      |
|        8. | Non Backlisted Undertaking            | H. 241      |
|        9. | Quality and ISO Certificates          | I. 246      |
|       10. | "Simila Work" confirming to GIGW norm | J. 312      |
|       11. | Fulltime Employee Undertaking         | K. 314      |
|       12. | Technical Proposal                    | L. 378      |
|       13. | All Other Forms                       | M. 403      |
|       14. | Signed RFP Documents                  | M. 403      |

![Image](data:image/png;base64,)

Logo

![Image](data:image/png;base64,)

Stamp

![Image](data:image/png;base64,)

![Image](data:image/png;base64,)

![Image](data:image/png;base64,)

![Image](data:image/png;base64,)

![Image](data:image/png;base64,)

![Image](data:image/png;base64,)

To, M/S. Power Finance Corporation Ltd. "Urjanidhi", 1, Braakhamba Lane, Connaught Place, New Delhi-110 001.

Subject: Procureement of services for Design, Development, Implementation and Maintenance of PFC Website Bid Number: GEM/2025/B/6305408 - 02:09:1977:24-25:Website, Dated: 04.06.2025

Dear Sir,

With reference to the release of the above-mentioned Tender, we are keen to participate and render our services to your esteemed organization. In this context, we are herewith submitting our proposal for the same.

Silver Touch Technologies Ltd. is a leading company providing IT services for various state governments, Government IT departments, central department and major PSUs. We have successfully executed large software, website, hardware and system integration projects during our last 25+ years of existence.

We have been accredited CMMi5, ISO 9001:2015, ISO 27001:2013, ISO 20000-1:2015 Certification for observing international standards in a wide scope of activities like Manufacturing, Sales &amp; Services of Peripheral &amp; Networking Products as well as Design &amp; Development of Software.

As per the tender requirements, we are herewith submitting the Bank Guarantee of Earnest Money Deposit (EMD) along with this tender.

We believe you will find our offer in line with your expectations; however, should you need any further information/clarifications, please feel free to contact us.

Thanking you.

For Silver Touch Technologies Limited,

![Image](data:image/png;base64,)

Divyant Sharma Manager - Bid Request, Bid Submission, Bid Approval, Bid Review, Bid Settlement, Bid Settlement

Divyant Sharma Manager - Bid Review, Bid Settlement, Bid Settlement

Divyant Sharma Manager - Bid Review, Bid Settlement, Bid Settlement

Divyant Sharma Manager - Bid Review, Bid Settlement, Bid Settlement

Divyant Sharma Manager - Bid Review, Bid Settlement, Bid Settlement

## CERTIFICATE OF INCORPORATION

No. 04 - 2446 of 1994-95

I hereby certify that silver touch

computers private limited is this day

incorporated under the Companies Act,

1956 (No.1 of 1956) and that the

Company is limited.

Given under my hand at AHMEDABAD this

second day of February, One thousand

Nineteenth five

of 1956, and that the

Company is limited.

Given under my hand at AHMEDABAD this

second day of February, One thousand

Nineteenth five

of 1956, and that the Company is limited.

Given under my hand at AHMEDABAD this

second day of February, One thousand

Nineteenth five

of 1956, and that the Company is limited.

![Image](data:image/png;base64,)

![Image](data:image/png;base64,)

Co. No. 044-24465

Fresh Certificate of Incorporation Consequent on

Change of Name

In the Office of

The Registrar of Companies,

Gujarat, Dadra and Nagar Haveli.

[Under the Companies Act, 1956 (1 of 1956)]

In the Matter of

Silvester Touch Computers Limited

Silvester Touch Computers Limited

which was originally incorporated on 02/02/1995 under the Companies Act, 1956

and under the name

Silvester Touch Computers Private Limited

having duly passed the necessary resolution in terms of Section 21 of the

Companies Act, 1956, on 17/05/2004 and the approval of the Central

signifies in writing having been accorded thereto by the Registrar of Companies,

Gujarat, vide his letter dated 13/10/2004, in terms of Government of India, Ministry of

of Law, Justice &amp; Company Affairs (Department of Company Affairs) Notification No.

No. GSR 50/E dated 24-06-1985 the name of the said Company is this day

changed to

Silvester Touch Technologies Limited

and this certificate is issued pursuant to Section 23(1) of the said Act.

Given under my hand at AHMEDABAD

Dated this 13th October, 2004.

Registrar of Companies,

Gujarat

Dadra &amp; Nagar Haveli

## Lease Agreement

THIS AGREEMENT is made at New Delhi this 1st day of September, 2023 BETWEEN Shri VRAJ VADEHRA (Aadhar No 2390 4986 8313) S/O Shri A.K. VADEHRA and R/O M-96, Greater Kailash Part - 2, New Delhi - 110048, hereinafter called "the Lessor" [which term so far as the context shall admit] shall mean and include his heirs, executors, administrators and permitted assigns] of the One Part AND M/s Silver Touch Technologies Limited, an Indian company duly registered under Indian companies Act, 1956 and having its Registered Office at Floor, Saffron Towers, Nr. Panchwati Circle, Ambawa, Ahmedabad - 380006 (Successors, assignees their authorized signatory Mr. Sanjay Roy (Aadhar No. 9906 7934 4939) , who has been duly authorized by a resolution passed by its Tender Investment and Finance Committee Board of Directors in the meeting held on 23/05/2023 (copy enclosed) - hereinafter referred to as "the Lessee". Which term, unless repugnant to the subject or context, shall mean and include their successors , assignees, executors and administrators of the second part.

WHEREAS:

![Image](data:image/png;base64,)

![Image](data:image/png;base64,)

140

occupy Flat No. 1-2-3 in the Building known as Rishyamook Building together with Three car parking space in the basement of the Building compound [hereinafter collectively called "the leased premises"], affairs of which Building are governed and managed by M/s Seth Associates as per by-laws and regulations framed by said Society.

[b] The Lessor presently is not in immediate need of licensed premises for his own use.

[c] The Lessee has requested the Lessor to grant the Lease of the said premises for the lawful commercial office use.which the Lessor has agreed to do upon the terms and conditions hereinafter mentioned.

NOW IT IS HEREBY AGREED by and between the parties hereto as follows:-

- 1. The Lessee by lease agreement of the Lessor hereby granted shall have the use and occupation of the leased premises for a fixed period of 36 months commencing from 1st June, 2003 and ending on 30th May, 2026 on the terms and conditions contained in this Agreement for the said User as aforesaid.
- 2. It is further agreed and accepted that after completion of every 12 months there will be an escalation of lease rent @ 5% PA on the last rent paid and is applicable and in force for the next 36 months i.e Rs.4,51,000/= (Rupees Four Lakhs Fifty One Thousand only ) plus GST, p. m. The rent for first year shall be Rs. 4,51,000/= (P.M. Plus GST @ 18%. (As applicable)

The rent for first year shall be Rs. 4,51,000/= (P.M. Plus GST @ 18%.

![Image](data:image/png;base64,)

![Image](data:image/png;base64,)

![Image](data:image/png;base64,)

The rent for second year shall be Rs. 4,73,550 /-PM Plus GST @

18%.

(As applicable)

And for the Third year

18%.

(As applicable)

- 3. The Lessee shall use the said demised premises for the purpose of and in connection with the lawful business of the Lessee. That during the continuance of the lease period, the Lessee shall not sublet, assign or otherwise part with the possession of the whole of the demised premises, or any part thereof to any third party.
- 4. The Lessee shall not be liable for any damage or loss resulting from the demised premises.
- 5. The Lessee shall pay the charges for the consumption of electricity and water charges in accordance with the bills received from the concerned authorities.
- 6. The Lessee shall not be liable for any damage or loss resulting from the demised premises.
- 7. The Lessee shall not be liable for any damage or loss resulting from the demised premises.
- 8. The Lessee shall not be liable for any damage or loss resulting from the demised premises.
- 9. The Lessee shall not be liable for any damage or loss resulting from the demised premises.
- 10. The Lessee shall not be liable for any damage or loss resulting from the demised premises.

![Image](data:image/png;base64,)

![Image](data:image/png;base64,)

![Image](data:image/png;base64,)

The above-mentioned act of the United States Congress, which was passed on January 1, 1861, and which was later amended by the United States Constitution, was the first act of the United States Congress. It was passed by the House of Representatives, which consisted of 11 members, 5 representatives from each state, and 4 representatives from the District of Columbia. The House of Representatives then passed the act to the Senate, which consisted of 10 members, 4 representatives from each state, and 3 representatives from the District of Columbia. The Senate then passed the act to the Governor, who was elected by the House of Representatives. The Governor then passed the act to the President, who was elected by the Senate.

The act of the United States Congress, which was passed on January 1, 1861, and which was later amended by the United States Constitution, was the first act of the United States Congress. It was passed by the House of Representatives, which consisted of 11 members, 5 representatives from each state, and 4 representatives from the District of Columbia. The House of Representatives then passed the act to the Senate, which consisted of 10 members, 4 representatives from each state, and 3 representatives from the District of Columbia. The Senate then passed the act to the Governor, who was elected by the House of Representatives. The Governor then passed the act to the President, who was elected by the Senate.

The act of the United States Congress, which was passed on January 1, 1861, and which was later amended by the United States Constitution, was the first act of the United States Congress. It was passed by the House of Representatives, which consisted of 11 members, 5 representatives from each state, and 4 representatives from the District of Columbia. The House of Representatives then passed the act to the Senate, which consisted of 10 members, 4 representatives from each state, and 3 representatives from the District of Columbia. The Senate then passed the act to the Governor, who was elected by the House of Representatives. The Governor then passed the act to the President, who was elected by the Senate.

The act of the United States Congress, which was passed on January 1, 1861, and which was later amended by the United States Constitution, was the first act of the United States Congress. It was passed by the House of Representatives, which consisted of 11 members, 5 representatives from each state, and 4 representatives from the District of Columbia. The House of Representatives then passed the act to the Senate, which consisted of 10 members, 4 representatives from each state, and 3 representatives from the District of Columbia. The Senate then passed the act to the Governor, who was elected by the House of Representatives. The Governor then passed the act to the President, who was elected by the Senate.

The act of the United States Congress, which was passed on January 1, 1861, and which was later amended by the United States Constitution, was the first act of the United States Congress. It was passed by the House of Representatives, which consisted of 11 members, 5 representatives from each state, and 4 representatives from the District of Columbia. The House of Representatives then passed the act to the Senate, which consisted of 10 members, 4 representatives from each state, and 3 representatives from the District of Columbia. The Senate then passed the act to the Governor, who was elected by the House of Representatives. The Governor then passed the act to the President, who was elected by the Senate.

The act of the United States Congress, which was passed on January 1, 1861, and which was later amended by the United States Constitution, was the first act of the United States Congress. It was passed by the House of Representatives, which consisted of 11 members, 5 representatives from each state, and 4 representatives from the District of Columbia. The House of Representatives then passed the act to the Senate, which consisted of 10 members, 4 representatives from each state, and 3 representatives from the District of Columbia. The Senate then passed the act to the Governor, who was elected by the House of Representatives. The Governor then passed the act to the President, who was elected by the Senate.

The act of the United States Congress, which was passed on January 1, 1861, and which was later amended by the United States Constitution, was the first act of the United States Congress. It was passed by the House of Representatives, which consisted of 11 members, 5 representatives from each state, and 4 representatives from the District of Columbia. The House of Representatives then passed the act to the Senate, which consisted of 10 members, 4 representatives from each state, and 3 representatives from the District of Columbia. The Senate then passed the act to the Governor, who was elected by the House of Representatives. The Governor then passed the act to the President, who was elected by the Senate.

![Image](data:image/png;base64,)

![Image](data:image/png;base64,)

- 9. The refundable security deposit remain the same, as per the previous rent agreement, Rs. 15,00,000 /= (Ruppes 15 lakhs) and as and by way of interest free refundable security deposit subject to other terms in these presents (said Deposit), the receipt whereof is hereto acknowledged by Lessor and which shall be refunded to the Lessee simultaneously at the time of the valid expiry or earlier termination or sooner determination of the Lease hereby granted, as provided in this Agreement. The refund of said Deposit will be made to aforesaid after deducting lawfarrows, if any, electricity charges and any other loss or damage caused to the leased premises subject to natural wear and tear and any other lawful charges due from Lessee in terms of and consonance with these presents. The Lessee shall at the same time against refund of said Deposit as aforesaid, handover the peaceful vacant possession to Lessor of the leased premises. The Lessee shall not try / or adjust the monthly rent payable through the Interest Free Security Deposit (IFSD) lying with the Lessor.
- 10. The Lessee shall during the validity of the Lease hereby granted, pay to the Lessor, lease rent or compensation of Rs. 4,51,000/= (Rupees Four Lakhs Fifty One Thousand) only plus GST as applicable under the law, presently which is 18 per month, which herein after called as "Lease Rent " for exclusively permitting the Lease to use the said leased premises. The Lessee shall accordingly during the tenure of Lease, pay the Lease rent as above on or before the 10th day of every calendar month ensuing from commencement of Lease, to the Lessor as lease rent or compensation as per these presents subject to deduction by the Lessee of tax at a source and any other statutory levy, cess etc., as applicable under the applicable laws e.g. Income Tax laws and the Lessee agrees to pay the tax etc., so deducted to the tax and concerned authorities and furnish prescribed 1.T. declaration as evidence of such payment to the

![Image](data:image/png;base64,)

![Image](data:image/png;base64,)

Lessor on or before 30th June of following the previous accounting year ended on 31st March.

- 11.The Lessee shall apart from Lease rent aforesaid be liable to pay actual charges for electricity consumed by the Lessee in the leased premises during the validity of Lease. The Lessee shall not be responsible for non-supply of any of the above utility only if the non-supply of the said utility is beyond the reasonable control of the lessor and is not due to the negligence or default of the lessor.
- 12.The Lessee shall bear and pay all existing and future taxes (including municipal taxes), cesses, levies, and other charges whatsoever [excluding Agency's maintenance, electricity and car parking charges] in respect of the licensed premises and/or in pursuance to these presents and all increases therein and shall indemnify the Lessee against the consequences of any breach of this clause and also if the Lessee's rights to use and occupy any property or property rights are infringed by the lessor, the lessor shall be liable for such infringement.
- 13.The Lessee has agreed and under taken the terms of this clause and agreed to do the normal repairs / renovation, fit out and paint and polish etc. and including wash rooms / toilets and pantry and A/C etc. at their own costs and expenses and the same shall not be claimed / charged from the lessor. Nor it shall be deducted from the monthly rent.

![Image](data:image/png;base64,)

![Image](data:image/png;base64,)

13 A. The Lessee shall use the leased premises with due care and caution subject to usual wear and tear and shall Erdeavour to keep the same in good order and condition subject to furthur to acts of God and situations beyond control of Lessee and situations not amounting to willful negligence on part of Lessee and upon the expiration or termination of the lease shall leave the same in as good condition as they were in or on the date hereof (reasonable wear and tear and loss or damage by fire, tempest, rains, accident, irresistible force or Act of God excepted and situations beyond control of Lessee and situations not amounting to willful negligence on part of Lessee) and make compensation in case of any damage willfully done to leased premises.

14. The Lessee shall not be responsible or liable in any manner whatsoever for any injury , theft or damage which may be caused to the Lessee or any employee of the User, guests, visitors, agents or others nor shall the Lessee be responsible for any theft, damage or destruction of the belongings, chattels, articles or things of the License or for any bodily injury to any person or her/their property during the occupancy of the licensed premises from any cause whatsoever unless caused due to negligence of the Lessee or any other person lying in the leased premises.

15. The Lessee shall also be responsible for all breakages in the said leased premises and be responsible for the maintenance of the interior thereof. The Lessee (with the Lesessor's written permission) shall be at liberty to make, fix or install additional fittings or fixtures in the licensed premises including air-conditioners, shelves, screens, racks, sunblinds, fittings and water, gas, electric, telephone, sanitary and other installations and lights and fans and to remove the same at any time and shall leave the leased premises as they were on the date.

![Image](data:image/png;base64,)

![Image](data:image/png;base64,)

hereof, subject to usual wear and tear and excepts as aforesaid. The Lessee shall however not do any structural alterations to the said leased premises.

- 16. The Lessee may, if it wishes, obtain at its own cost any insurance cover in respect of Lessee's furniture, chattels, effects, valuables and/or the Lessee's furniture, fixtures and fittings in the leased premises. The Lessee hereby takes note of the fact and acknowledges that the Lessee carries no insurance cover for any property of the Lessee. However, on account of Insuranc e cover taken by said Society or to its employees, if any loss or damage to the Lessee's articles or loss to its employees, directors', servants or agents can be recovered, the benefits thereof shall be deemed to have accrued to Lessee and payments against such same if received by Lessee or said Society, the Lessee shall be entitled to receive the same from them.
- 18.The Lessee shall not do or cause or allow or permit to be done in or around the leased premises or in any other part of the said building, anything of an illegal or immoral nature or which may be a willful nuisance or annoyance or be lawfully objectionable to the said Society or the other occupants of the said building or prejudice the rights of the lessee as the owner of the leased premises.
- 19.The Lessee shall not store or allow to be stored in the leased premises any goods, articles or things of hazardous nature or combustible nature. The Lessee shall not make any alteration or addition to the external portions/areas of any part of the leased premises in any manner whatsoever.
- 20.The Lessee shall not make any alteration or addition to the external portions/areas of any part of the leased premises in any manner whatsoever.
- 21. The Lessee shall not make any alterations or additions to the leased premises in any manner whatsoever.

![Image](data:image/png;base64,)

![Image](data:image/png;base64,)

![Image](data:image/png;base64,)

Lessor shall be and shall always be deemed to be license and only the Lessor shall be and shall always be deemed to be in exclusive legal possession and in full charge and control of the leased premises at all times.

29.Stamp duty and registration fees and miscellaneous charges payable on this Agreement shall be borne equally by both the parties.

30.The Lessor shall be entitled to enter upon the said leased premises at any time by giving 24 hours prior written notice to the Lessee and thereupon the Lessor and the Lessor's duly authorized representative shall have full liberty to inspect the said premises at any reasonable time and to effect any such repairs as the Lessor is required to do pursuant to her or her agents' custody or custody of the premises.

35.No addition or alterations or modification to these presents claimed by any party herein shall be binding on the other party unless same is recorded in writing duly executed by both parties and/or their duly authorized and empowered representatives.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF THE PARTIES HERETO HAVE EXECUTED THIS AGREEMENT.

In duplicate, the date and year first hereinafter written.

SIGNED AND DELIVERED

Signature:

Mr. Viraj Vadehra (Lessor)

Other

## ANNEXURE - "A"

List of All Fittings and Fixtures provided at Flat No. 1, 2, 3, at 1st Floor of Rishyamook Building, 85A, Panchkuin Road, New Delhi-110 001.
